Reactor Conversion %
==================================================

.. math::

   \text{}

.. raw:: html

   <table class="kpi-table">
      <tbody>
         <tr>
            <td>Description</td>
            <td><ul><li>Reactor Conversion % measures the efficiency of a chemical reactor by calculating the percentage of feed material that has been converted into product</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Objective</td>
            <td><ul><li>The objective of monitoring Reactor Conversion % is to optimize reactor performance</li><li>enhance yield</li><li>and minimize waste in the production process</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Formula</td>
            <td>
               <p>The formula for Reactor Conversion % is derived by dividing the amount of converted material by the total feed amount, then multiplying by 100 to express it as a percentage.</p>
               <p>See code context for implementation details.</p>
            </td>
         </tr>

         <tr>
            <td>Generation time</td>
            <td>
               0.0s (AI 0.0s + Render 0.0s)
               <div style="font-size: 12px; color: #6b7280; margin-top: 4px;">
                  Total: 0.0s
                  • AI: 0.0s
                  • Render: 0.0s
               </div>
            </td>
         </tr>

         <tr>
            <td>Extraction rate %</td>
            <td>88.9%</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Error rate %</td>
            <td>11.1%</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Used in KPI(s)</td>
            <td><ul><li>Reactor Conversion % is utilized in key performance indicators related to production efficiency</li><li>yield optimization</li><li>and process effectiveness</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Input Measure</td>
            <td><ul><li>The input measures for this KPI include the total feed amount and the quantity of material that has been converted during the reaction</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Unit of Measure</td>
            <td>The unit of measure for Reactor Conversion % is percentage.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Reporting Source</td>
            <td>The reporting source for this KPI is typically derived from operational data collected during the chemical reaction process.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Comments</td>
            <td>A high Reactor Conversion % indicates effective reactor operation, while a low percentage may signal issues such as incomplete reactions or suboptimal conditions.</td>
         </tr>
      </tbody>
   </table>

.. admonition:: Code Context
   :class: dropdown

   
   
   

   .. code-block:: sql
      :linenos:
   
      :emphasize-lines: 1
   

      -- KPI: Reactor Conversion %
   SELECT 100.0 * (converted / NULLIF(feed,0)) AS [Reactor Conversion %]
   FROM (SELECT 950 AS converted, 1000 AS feed) a;
